response.justification: Bogdan brings genuine, current hands-on experience building AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling at re:cinq, which directly aligns with the AI-native engineering environment the role treats as a baseline. His TypeScript depth is solid, his TDD and software craftsmanship ethos matches the ownership mindset required, and his time at Sociomantic demonstrates real high-throughput distributed systems credibility at scale. The critical blocker is Python: it is entirely absent from his skills list, employment history, and side projects, despite being listed as a must-have for the role. His distributed systems work is also several years dated, and the resume lacks any explicit signal around on-call ownership or incident response.
strengths: Current, hands-on AI-agent and MCP infrastructure work at re:cinq — rare and direct alignment with the AI-native engineering environment the role treats as a baseline expectation
Demonstrated high-throughput distributed systems credibility at Sociomantic, processing millions of HTTP requests per second using async I/O, caching, fibers, and low-latency design
Consistent ownership mindset across a long career: solo takeover of iOS at WatchUp, decade-long stewardship of GISCollective, Staff titles at multiple organizations
concerns: Python is completely absent from every section of the resume — skills, employment history, and projects — yet it is listed as a must-have technical skill for this role
High-throughput distributed systems work is real but dated; Sociomantic ended in 2020 and recent years skew toward product-level TypeScript/Node.js rather than distributed infrastructure
No mention of on-call rotations, SLO/SLA ownership, or incident-response experience, all of which the role explicitly requires as operational readiness
